edges.description: Section A describes the general design and function of the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV) as a pressure-retaining component of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS), while Section B references the inspection, shipment, and installation requirements that ensure the RPV meets applicable codes and standards.

Section A describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, while Section B details compliance of RPV attachments and welds with ASME BPVC requirements, providing evidence that the RPV design and fabrication meet regulatory standards for pressure containment.

Section A describes the physical design and function of the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, while Section B addresses the operational procedures developed to ensure the RPV operates within its pressure-temperature limits, thus connecting the vessel's design parameters (from Table 5.3-1) to the procedural controls that maintain its integrity under design conditions.

Section A describes the RPV as a pressure-retaining component of the RCS, while Section B references the inspection and handling requirements that ensure the RPV's integrity as described in Section A.

Section A describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, while Section B discusses compliance of RPV attachments and welds with ASME BPVC requirements, thus Section B provides evidence that supports the adequacy of the RPV design described in Section A.

Section A describes the physical design and function of the RPV as a pressure-retaining component of the RCS, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure the RPV operates within pressure-temperature limits, connecting the vessel's design to its operational safety requirements.

Section A describes inspection, shipment, and installation requirements for the RPV referencing ASME codes, while Section B details compliance of RPV attachments and welds with specific ASME BPVC requirements, both providing evidence that the RPV design and fabrication processes adhere to regulatory standards for structural integrity.

Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits (from Table 5.3-1) are not exceeded, while Section A references the inspection and handling requirements that ensure the RPV is built and maintained to meet those same design parameters, thus both sections collectively support the adequacy of the RPV design for containing the RCS at design conditions.

Section A describes the ASME code compliance and examination requirements for RPV attachments, while Section B explains that operating procedures are developed to ensure pressure-temperature limits (as defined by code and analyses like those in Section A) are not exceeded, thus connecting the design/code compliance in A with the operational controls in B.

Section A describes the overall design and components of the Reactor Coolant System, including the RPV, while Section B provides specific details about the RPV as a pressure-retaining component within that system, connecting them through the RPV's role and function in the RCS.

Section A describes the design and components of the RPV within the RCS, while Section B details the RPV's role as a pressure-retaining component of the RCS, with both sections connecting through their discussion of the RPV's function and integration in the RCS at design conditions.

Section A describes the RPV's design features and intended functions, while Section B specifies the inspection, shipment, and installation requirements that ensure the RPV is constructed and maintained according to regulatory standards, thereby supporting the adequacy of the RPV design for containing the RCS at design conditions.

Section A specifies the materials and components of the RPV upper shell and head, which are part of the pressurizer, while Section B provides the overall description and function of the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, connecting the detailed material information in A to the general vessel function in B.

Section A references Section B by indicating that the material of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are part of the pressurizer, is described in Section 5.2, while Section B provides the general description of the RPV as a pressure-retaining component of the RCS.

Section A specifies the materials and components of the RPV upper shell and head, which are relevant to the design adequacy assessed using parameters in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1, while Section B details the inspection and handling requirements for the RPV, ensuring that the materials and fabrication described in Section A are properly verified and maintained.

Section A defines the integrity requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), which includes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) described in Section B as a pressure-retaining component of the reactor coolant system (RCS), thus establishing that the RPV's adequacy is directly relevant to satisfying the RCPB integrity requirements.

Section B describes the RPV as a pressure-retaining component of the RCS, which Section A identifies as part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity must be maintained, thus Section B provides specific details about a component referenced in Section A's general boundary definition.

Section A outlines the integrity requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), which includes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies the inspection, shipment, and installation requirements for the RPV to ensure it meets those integrity standards.

Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, which must withstand the design conditions specified in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1, while Section A discusses the DHRS's role in maintaining RCS conditions within acceptable limits, thereby supporting the adequacy of the RPV design.

Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining boundary for the RCS, which is the system whose heat removal and pressure control are addressed by the DHRS design basis in Section A, connecting the vessel's material adequacy to the system's ability to maintain safe conditions at design parameters.

Section B describes the inspection and handling requirements for the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which are prerequisites to ensure the RPV's integrity before evaluating its adequacy for containing the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) at design conditions as discussed in the analysis question referencing Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1.

Section A states that the RPV is designed and constructed to meet ASME Code Class 1 requirements per 10 CFR 50.55a, while Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, thus connecting the regulatory compliance of the RPV (A) to its functional role in containing the RCS (B).

Section A states that the RPV is designed and constructed to meet ASME Code Class 1 requirements per 10 CFR 50.55a, while Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, connecting the regulatory compliance to the physical function of the vessel.

Section A states that the RPV is designed and constructed in compliance with ASME BPVC Section III, while Section B specifies that shipment, handling, and storage of the RPV also adhere to ASME BPVC Section III and related standards, thus Section B provides detailed implementation supporting Section A's general compliance claim.

Section A provides a general description of the RPV's design and its components, while Section B specifies detailed material information for the pressurizer, which is part of the RPV described in Section A.

Section A describes the specific design and components of the RPV, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, connecting them through the need for the RPV design (A) to satisfy the integrity requirements (B) for safe operation.

Section A describes the overall design and components of the RPV, including the DHRS as a subsystem, while Section B provides the specific design basis and function of the DHRS, which is one of the systems housed within the RPV described in Section A.

Section A describes the RPV's design features and components, while Section B states that the RPV is designed and constructed in compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thus Section A's design details are intended to satisfy Section B's regulatory standards.

Section A provides specific material details and component descriptions for parts of the RPV and pressurizer, which are referenced in Section B's general discussion of the RCPB's design features and regulatory consistency.

Section A specifies the materials and components of the RPV and pressurizer, while Section B describes the DHRS's function to remove decay heat from the RCS, with both sections connected through their roles in ensuring the RPV can withstand design conditions during heat removal scenarios.

Section A describes the specific materials and components of the RPV and pressurizer, while Section B states that these components are designed and constructed in compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, connecting the design details to regulatory adequacy.

Section A describes the integrity requirements and design features of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (including the RPV), while Section B outlines the decay heat removal system's role in maintaining safe RCS conditions, both of which are connected by their shared reliance on RCS design parameters (such as those in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1) to ensure the RPV can contain the RCS at design conditions.

Section A describes the design intent to maintain RCPB integrity, while Section B specifies that this integrity is achieved by complying with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thus Section B provides the regulatory basis that supports the claims made in Section A.

Section A describes the design basis and function of the Decay Heat Removal System in maintaining safe RCS conditions, while Section B establishes that the RPV and associated components are designed and constructed to meet regulatory and ASME code requirements, thereby connecting the system's operational adequacy (A) to its compliance with design standards (B).

Section A specifies the material standards and regulatory compliance for the RPV, which are referenced in Section B's description of the RPV as a pressure-retaining component of the RCS, thus linking the adequacy of RPV design to its material conformance.

Section A specifies that the RPV materials conform to ASME and regulatory requirements, while Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, connecting the material compliance in A to the functional role in B.

Section A specifies that the RCPB materials, including those for the RPV, conform to ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B references the handling, shipment, and storage requirements for the RPV as per ASME BPVC and NQA-1 standards, thus Section B provides detailed procedural requirements that support the general material compliance described in Section A.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state parameters for optimal reactor system performance, while Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component designed to contain the RCS under those parameters, connecting the design values in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1 to the vessel's adequacy.

Section A discusses the evaluation of system parameters for optimal performance, while Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, connecting the system evaluation context to the vessel's design function.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state parameters for system performance, which are the design values referenced in Table 5.3-1, while Section B addresses the inspection, shipment, and installation requirements for the RPV, ensuring it is built and maintained to meet those design parameters.

Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, while Section A references the RCS and its operational conditions, establishing that the adequacy of the RPV design (Section B) is directly related to the RCS design conditions (Section A).

Section A describes operational procedures and system components that interact with the RPV, while Section B provides the structural and material details of the RPV as the pressure-retaining boundary for the RCS, connecting them through the RPV's role in containing RCS pressure under design conditions.

Section B specifies the inspection and handling requirements for the RPV, which are necessary prerequisites to ensure the system components described in Section A function as intended under design conditions.

Section A outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a) that the RPV must comply with for overpressure protection, while Section B describes the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS whose design must adhere to those codes.

Section A outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) governing the design and overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary, which Section B applies specifically to the RPV as the pressure-retaining component of the RCS, thereby linking the general regulatory requirements to the specific vessel design.

Section A specifies the ASME BPVC and regulatory code requirements that the RPV design and fabrication must comply with, while Section B references the application of these same ASME BPVC standards during the shipment, handling, and storage of the RPV, thus Section B provides details for Section A's general compliance framework.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state parameters for optimal system performance, which are directly informed by the component-level operational details described in Section B, such as pressure and water level management in the RPV and PZR.

Section A discusses the establishment of design parameters for optimal reactor system performance, while Section B outlines compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory codes for the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, connecting them through the requirement that the design parameters (from Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1) must satisfy the regulatory and code-based integrity requirements.

Section A describes operational procedures and system component interactions that affect reactor pressure vessel (RPV) conditions, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the RPV design must comply with, connecting them through the requirement that operational states must not violate code-based design limits.
